componentDescription: Popover is a click-triggered floating panel for richer content than a tooltip — forms, filters, date pickers, color pickers, and contextual menus. Unlike Modal, it doesn't block the page. Unlike Tooltip, it's click-triggered and can contain interactive elements.
compositionPattern: Pass a trigger element and content element. The trigger is rendered in place; the content floats as a positioned overlay. For complex interactive content (forms, filter panels), manage open state with onOpenChange.

usageExamples.title: Filter popover
usageExamples.description: Button that opens a filter configuration panel.

trigger.llmContext: Required. The element that the user clicks to open the popover. Can be any React node — typically a Button, icon button, or text. The trigger is rendered inline in the DOM.
trigger.propDescription: Required. Element that triggers the popover on click

content.llmContext: Required. The content rendered inside the floating popover panel. Can be any React node including forms, lists, and interactive elements. Keep it focused and reasonably sized — use a Modal for large complex content.
content.propDescription: Required. Content rendered inside the floating popover

open.llmContext: Controlled open state. Pair with onOpenChange for external control. Use controlled mode when you need to open/close the popover programmatically from outside (e.g., after a save action, from a keyboard shortcut).
open.propDescription: Controlled open state of the popover

onOpenChange.llmContext: Callback fired with the new boolean open state. Use to sync with controlled open state or to run side effects when the popover opens/closes.
onOpenChange.propDescription: Called with new open state when popover opens or closes

closeOnOutsideClick.llmContext: Default true. Set to false when the popover contains forms or actions that should not be accidentally dismissed (e.g., a popover with unsaved filter changes). Remember to provide an explicit close mechanism.
closeOnOutsideClick.propDescription: Whether clicking outside closes the popover

side.llmContext: 'bottom' is the most common for button-triggered popovers. Use 'top' if the button is near the bottom of the screen. 'left' or 'right' for side-panel adjacent triggers. The popover auto-flips when there's insufficient space.
side.propDescription: Preferred side to display the popover
